title: COVID–19 Vaccination Non-Discrimination Act
plain_summary: This bill makes a rule about COVID-19 vaccines and medical care. It says that hospitals and doctors cannot refuse to treat patients just because they did or did not get a COVID-19 vaccine. If medical places do refuse patients for this reason, the government will stop giving them money. This includes money from Medicare, Medicaid, and children's health insurance programs.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the COVID-19 Vaccination Non-Discrimination Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition on Federal Funds for Entities That Refuse to Provide Treatment Based on COVID-19 Vaccination Status
sections.text: No matter what any other law says, the federal government cannot give money to any medical facility that refuses to treat someone because of whether or not they got a COVID-19 vaccine. This rule applies to all money that Congress has approved for spending by federal law. It also applies to money in special government accounts called trust funds that get federal money. This includes money from Medicare (health insurance for people sixty-five and older), Medicaid (health insurance for low-income people), and the Children's Health Insurance Program. If a facility turns away patients just because they are vaccinated or not vaccinated against COVID-19, that facility cannot receive any of this federal money.
